seoTitle: Jimbour East vs Jimna — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Jimbour East and Jimna. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Jimbour East vs Jimna: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Jimbour East and Jimna.
heading: Jimbour East vs Jimna — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Jimbour East, Queensland and Jimna, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jimbour East has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Jimna (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Jimbour East or Jimna safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Jimbour East has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Jimna scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Jimbour East scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Jimna scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Jimbour East or Jimna?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jimbour East has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
